Name: mixed_precision_kernel_tuning
Title: Kernel-Tuned Mixed-Precision Quantization for Enhanced GPU Inference Efficiency
Short Hypothesis: Can we systematically optimize mixed-precision quantization at the kernel level to achieve significant improvements in inference latency and throughput on commodity GPUs?
Related Work: While recent works like KVTuner and Cocktail focus on mixed-precision quantization for KV caches and long-context models, they do not address the detailed kernel-level optimizations necessary for efficient inference. This proposal distinguishes itself by targeting kernel-specific tuning and profiling to achieve real-world latency improvements, which is often overlooked in theoretical frameworks.
Abstract: This research proposes a novel framework for Kernel-Tuned Mixed-Precision Quantization (KTMPQ) that integrates kernel-level optimization strategies with mixed-precision quantization for deep learning models. By focusing on optimizing the CUDA kernels involved in the inference process, this framework systematically identifies optimal bit-width configurations tailored to specific layers and operations. We aim to demonstrate that through profiling and tuning of kernel performance metrics—such as occupancy, bandwidth utilization, and arithmetic intensity—we can achieve substantial improvements in inference latency and throughput on commodity GPUs. Our approach will leverage empirical tuning of heuristic parameters based on measured performance, contrasting with existing methods that often rely on static configurations. We will validate the effectiveness of our framework across various model architectures, reporting improvements in both real-time inference and energy efficiency.
Experiments: 1. Implement a baseline mixed-precision quantization framework and evaluate its performance on selected models using standard metrics.
2. Develop a kernel profiling tool to measure occupancy, bandwidth, and latency for different quantization configurations across various layers.
3. Conduct A/B testing of different mixed-precision configurations, focusing on kernel-level optimizations, with metrics including p50/p99 latency and throughput under batching.
4. Analyze the impact of specific kernel optimizations (e.g., memory coalescing, register usage) on overall model performance, documenting before and after traces to isolate gains.
Risk Factors and Limitations: 1. The effectiveness of kernel-level tuning may vary across different GPU architectures, potentially limiting generalizability.
2. Complexity in profiling and tuning kernels could lead to longer development times, which might affect the overall timeline for results.
3. The approach may require extensive empirical testing to identify the optimal configurations, which could be resource-intensive.
